task_completion.description: Fetch and load the 1KF1 structure into the molecular visualization software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL viewer or object panel clearly shows the '1KF1' structure loaded.

task_completion.description: Create a visualization showing the DNA backbone and the guanine bases forming the tetrads as sticks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer displays the DNA structure with the specific guanine residues (DG) rendered in a stick representation.

task_completion.description: Render the centrally bound potassium ions as spheres.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer shows spherical representations of the potassium (K) atoms located in the central channel of the G-quadruplex.

task_completion.description: Identify and measure distances between potassium ions and coordinating guanine O6 oxygen atoms (within 3.5 Angstroms).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dashed measurement lines with distance values (typically between 2.4 and 3.5 Angstroms) connecting the central potassium spheres to the oxygen atoms of the surrounding guanine sticks.

task_completion.description: Save a high-resolution PNG figure of the visualization to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command, PyMOL command line, or file explorer showing the image being saved to or existing at '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/g_quadruplex.png'.

task_completion.description: Write a report containing the potassium summary, coordinating guanine residues, and at least 8 explicit distance measurements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/k_coordination_report.txt', including a list of residue numbers (e.g., 2, 3, 4, 8...) and at least 8 distance values.

integrity.description: The agent used a molecular visualization tool (like PyMOL) to analyze the structure and generate the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the PyMOL GUI open with the 1KF1 structure actively loaded and manipulated, rather than just a script being written.

integrity.description: The agent extracted real distance measurements from the software rather than hallucinating them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The distance values written in the text report match the measurements visible in the PyMOL viewer or PyMOL console output.

integrity.description: The agent actually created the required files in the specified directories.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal commands (like 'ls' or 'cat') or file manager windows show the files 'g_quadruplex.png' and 'k_coordination_report.txt' existing in their respective '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/' directories.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses PDB ID 1KF1. The known coordinating guanine (DG) tetrad residues are: 2, 3, 4, 8, 9, 10, 14, 15, 16, 20, 21, 22. The maximum coordination distance is 3.5 Angstroms. Note: The true minimum distance in the structure is ~2.434 A (between K24 and DG10), so measurements around 2.4-3.5 A are correct and should not be penalized. The figure must be saved to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/g_quadruplex.png and the report to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/k_coordination_report.txt with at least 8 distance measurements.
